zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Bruce於1317年出生在蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林時，她的父親Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots43歲，母親Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots43歲。她和Walter Oliphant至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1364年在蘇格蘭享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Bruce于1317年出生在苏格兰費伏登福姆林时，她的父亲Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots43岁，母亲Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots43岁。她和Walter Oliphant至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1364年在苏格兰享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bruce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from a place in Normandy, probably from Brix (La Manche).

History: The son of the Domesday baron, a friend of David I of Scotland, was granted by him the Lordship of Annandale in 1124, and his second son Robert became the founder of the Scottish house of Bruce. — This surname (see 2 above) is listed along with its original form Brousse in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Brousse
meanings: French: topographic name for someone living in a scrubby area, from Old French bro(u)ce ‘thicket, brushwood’ (from Late Latin bruscia), or a habitational name from any of several places in France named Brousse. Compare Bruce and Brusse .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Bruce) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Brewis
meanings: (of Norman origin): habitational name from Briouze in Orne.
(Northumberland and Durham): topographic name or nickname from Middle English breuhous ‘brewery’ (literally ‘brew house’).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Bruce was born in 1317, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, her father, Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots, was 43 and her mother, Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots, was 43. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Walter Oliphant. She died in 1364, in Scotland, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Bruce est née en 1317, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, son père, Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots, avait 43 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Walter Oliphant. Elle est décédée en 1364, à Écosse, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Bruce 1317 in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots 43 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots 43. Sie hatte mit Walter Oliphant mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1364 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Bruce è nata il nel 1317 nel Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, sua padre, Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots, aveva 43 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Walter Oliphant. Morì nel 1364, nel Scotland, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Bruceが1317年にスコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインで生まれた時，父Robert I the Bruce, King of Scotsは43歳，母Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scotsは43歳であった。彼女はWalter Oliphantとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1364年にスコットランドで47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Bruce(이)가 1317년 스컷랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 Walter Oliphant(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1364년 스컷랜드에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Bruce nasceu em 1317, em Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, seu pai, Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots, tinha 43 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Walter Oliphant. Ela faleceu em 1364, em Scotland, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Bruce родилась в 1317 г. в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots, 43. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Walter Oliphant. Она умерла в 1364 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Bruce nació en 1317, en Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, su papá, Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots, tenía 43 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Walter Oliphant. Murió en 1364, en Scotland, a la edad de 47 años.
